factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different segments, with coal facing challenges while automotive and intermodal show growth.

evidence.text: The reduction in earnings was primarily due to a 9% decline in income from railway operations. First-quarter operating results were negatively impacted by continued weakness in coal markets, lower average revenue per unit related to the mix of traffic, and additional costs attributable to harsh weather and service recovery efforts.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Mixed demand with declines in coal offset by growth in automotive and intermodal.

factors.summary: Pricing power is weak due to lower fuel surcharge revenues and competitive pressures.

evidence.text: The decline in revenue resulted from lower average revenue per unit (primarily a result of lower fuel surcharges as well as changes in the mix of traffic) offset in part by higher volumes.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Weak pricing due to lower fuel surcharge revenues and competitive pressures.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments as coal revenues decline and intermodal shows slight improvement.

evidence.text: First quarter railway operating revenues were $2.6 billion in 2015 , down $122 million , or 5% , compared with the first quarter of 2014 .  As shown in the following table, the decline in revenue resulted from lower average revenue per unit (primarily a result of lower fuel surcharges as well as changes in the mix of traffic) offset in part by higher volumes.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shifting towards lower-margin segments with coal decline.

factors.summary: Organic growth is decelerating due to lower coal volumes and fuel surcharge revenue, despite higher volumes in some segments.

evidence.text: First quarter 2015 net income was $310 million , down 16% compared with the same period last year.  The decrease in net income for the quarter reflected lower income from railway operations, down $61 million , or 9% , primarily due to a $122 million , or 5% , decline in railway operating revenues as a result of lower average revenue per unit (driven primarily by the decline in fuel surcharge revenue) and lower coal volumes.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decelerating growth due to lower coal volumes and fuel surcharge revenue.

factors.summary: Volumes are growing in some segments (e.g., automotive, intermodal) but declining in others (e.g., coal), leading to mixed trends.

evidence.text: First quarter 2015 net income was $310 million , down 16% compared with the same period last year.  The decrease in net income for the quarter reflected lower income from railway operations, down $61 million , or 9% , primarily due to a $122 million , or 5% , decline in railway operating revenues as a result of lower average revenue per unit (driven primarily by the decline in fuel surcharge revenue) and lower coal volumes.

factors.summary: Coal markets are weakening, while automotive and intermodal are showing growth.

evidence.text: Coal revenues decreased $86 million , or 16% , in the first quarter, compared with the same period last year, reflecting a 9% decrease in average revenue per unit (including fuel surcharges) and a 7% decline in carload volume. [...] Intermodal revenues declined $4 million, or 1%, in the first quarter , compared with the same period last year as a 5% decline in average revenue per unit, the result of lower fuel surcharges, was mostly offset by a 5% improvement in volume.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Weakening coal markets despite growth in automotive and intermodal.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel expenses, are declining due to lower oil prices, but this is offset by other factors.

evidence.text: Fuel expense, which includes the cost of locomotive fuel as well as other fuel used in railway operations, decreased $168 million, or 39%, in the quarter, primarily the result of a 41% drop in locomotive fuel prices.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Declining input costs due to lower oil prices.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher pay rates, payroll taxes, and overtime.

evidence.text: Compensation and benefits expense increased $43 million, or 6%, in the first quarter , reflecting: [...] •pay rates, up $26 million, [...] •employee levels, including increased overtime, up $14 million.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increasing labor costs due to higher pay rates and overtime.

factors.summary: Margins are compressing due to declining revenues and rising labor and purchased services expenses.

evidence.text: The railway operating ratio (a measure of the amount of operating revenues consumed by operating expenses) rose to 76.4% for the first quarter of 2015 , compared with 75.2% for the first quarter of 2014 .

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are compressing due to declining revenues and rising costs.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions are partially offsetting the impact of lower fuel prices, but expenses are still rising due to other factors.

evidence.text: Higher costs associated with increased intermodal and merchandise volumes and additional resources aimed at improving our service partially offset the reduction in fuel costs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction actions are partially effective but expenses are still rising.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, but challenges remain due to harsh weather and service recovery efforts.

evidence.text: Our network fluidity is improving, and we expect our metrics (train speed and terminal dwell) to approach 2012-2013 performance during the second half of the year.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving despite challenges.

factors.summary: Management expects coal revenues to remain lower for the remainder of the year, while intermodal revenues are expected to grow.

evidence.text: Coal revenues for the remainder of the year are expected to be lower compared to last year due to lower volumes and lower average revenue per unit (largely a result of lower fuel surcharge revenue). [...] Intermodal revenues for the remainder of the year are expected to be higher compared to last year due to volume increases, offset in part by lower average revenue per unit (driven by lower fuel surcharge revenue).

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed outlook with coal revenues declining but intermodal growth expected.

factors.summary: Management is cautious due to coal market weakness and lower fuel surcharge revenues, but remains optimistic about intermodal growth.

evidence.text: Our network fluidity is improving, and we expect our metrics (train speed and terminal dwell) to approach 2012-2013 performance during the second half of the year. [...] Intermodal revenues for the remainder of the year are expected to be higher compared to last year due to volume increases, offset in part by lower average revenue per unit (driven by lower fuel surcharge revenue).
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management is cautious but optimistic about certain areas.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include continued coal market weakness, harsh weather impacts, and competition in intermodal.

evidence.text: The reduction in earnings was primarily due to a 9% decline in income from railway operations. First-quarter operating results were negatively impacted by continued weakness in coal markets, lower average revenue per unit related to the mix of traffic, and additional costs attributable to harsh weather and service recovery efforts. These factors more than offset the effects of volume increases and lower fuel prices.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include market weakness, weather impacts, and competition.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on improving network fluidity and service, with investments in infrastructure and intermodal growth.

evidence.text: Our network fluidity is improving, and we expect our metrics (train speed and terminal dwell) to approach 2012-2013 performance during the second half of the year. [...] Intermodal revenues for the remainder of the year are expected to be higher compared to last year due to volume increases, offset in part by lower average revenue per unit (driven by lower fuel surcharge revenue).
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Long-term focus on improving service and infrastructure.

factors.summary: Sensitivities include fuel prices, which significantly impact revenue and expenses, and labor relations.

evidence.text: Over 85% of our revenue base is covered by negotiated fuel surcharges, and revenues associated with these surcharges totaled $163 million and $295 million in the first quarters of 2015 and 2014 , respectively. [...] We largely bargain nationally in concert with other major railroads, represented by the National Carriers Conference Committee (NCCC). Moratorium provisions in the labor agreements govern when the railroads and the unions may propose changes to the agreements.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivities to fuel prices and labor relations present risks but also opportunities.

factors.summary: A potential catalyst is the expected resolution of rate reasonableness complaints, which could positively impact revenues.

evidence.text: Two of our customers, DuPont and Sunbelt Chlor Alkai Partnership (Sunbelt), filed rate reasonableness complaints before the Surface Transportation Board (STB) alleging that our tariff rates for transportation of regulated movements are unreasonable. [...] The STB’s findings in both cases remain subject to technical corrections, requests for reconsideration, and appeal. We believe the estimate of any reasonably possible loss will not have a material effect on our financial position, results of operations, or liquidity.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The potential resolution of rate reasonableness complaints could positively impact revenues.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is improving, but challenges from harsh weather and service recovery efforts are impacting performance.

evidence.text: Our network fluidity is improving, and we expect our metrics (train speed and terminal dwell) to approach 2012-2013 performance during the second half of the year.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving network fluidity and expected performance metrics indicate positive developments.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is expected to improve, particularly in intermodal and general merchandise segments.

evidence.text: Intermodal revenues for the remainder of the year are expected to be higher compared to last year due to volume increases, offset in part by lower average revenue per unit (driven by lower fuel surcharge revenue).

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expected improvement in capacity utilization, particularly in key segments, suggests growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are stable, with no significant risks of obsolescence or shortage.

evidence.text: Cash and cash equivalents totaled $524 million at March 31, 2015 , and were invested in accordance with our corporate investment policy as approved by the Finance Committee of our Board of Directors. The portfolio contains securities that are subject to market risk.  There are no limits or restrictions on our access to the assets.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable inventory levels with no significant risks indicate a neutral stance.

factors.summary: Quality and safety are stable, with no significant incidents reported.

evidence.text: Quality and safety are stable, with no significant incidents reported in the provided text.

sentiment.rationale: Stable quality and safety with no incidents reported suggest a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Market share is stable with no indication of significant gains or losses.

evidence.text: Market share is stable with no indication of significant gains or losses in the provided text.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Market share stability without significant gains or losses indicates neutrality.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are challenging, with pressure from new entrants and aggressive rivals in intermodal and general merchandise.

evidence.text: Export coal tonnage declined 18% in the first quarter of 2015, driven by strong competition that U.S. coal suppliers faced due to excess coal supply, depressed coal prices, and an Australian currency advantage.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Challenging competitive dynamics with pressure from new entrants and aggressive rivals pose a risk.

factors.summary: Differentiation is maintained through operational efficiency and intermodal network strength.

evidence.text: We operate the most extensive intermodal network in the East and are a major transporter of coal, automotive, and industrial products.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency and strong intermodal network provide a competitive advantage, indicating a positive sentiment.

sentiment.rationale: The customer concentration is stable with no significant changes in diversification.

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is ongoing, with a focus on efficiency and service improvement.

evidence.text: In January 2015 we reached an agreement covering wages and work rules through 2019 with the Brotherhood of Locomotive Engineers and Trainmen (BLET), which represents approximately 4,600 of our locomotive engineers. Any changes to BLET benefits will be bargained nationally through the NCCC.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Headcount restructuring is ongoing, but there's no indication of positive or negative outcome.

evidence.text: More than 80% of our railroad employees are covered by collective bargaining agreements with various labor unions. [...] Separately, in January 2015 we reached an agreement covering wages and work rules through 2019 with the Brotherhood of Locomotive Engineers and Trainmen (BLET), which represents approximately 4,600 of our locomotive engineers.

sentiment.label: neutral

factors.summary: Capex is expected to increase, driven by investments in freight cars and containers.

evidence.text: Cash used in investing activities was $378 million for the first quarter of 2015 , compared with $254 million in the same period last year, primarily reflecting the absence of short-term investment maturities. [...] We committed to purchase approximately $160 million of freight cars and containers.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable, with a total debt-to-total capitalization ratio of 42.3%.

evidence.text: Our total debt-to-total capitalization ratio was 42.3% at March 31, 2015 , and 42.1% at December 31, 2014 .

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are strong, with significant share repurchases and consistent dividend payments.

evidence.text: In the first quarter of 2015 , we repurchased 3.9 million  shares of Common Stock at a total cost of $415 million . [...] Cash provided by operating activities for the first quarter of 2015 totaled $605 million , which along with cash on hand allowed for share repurchases, property additions, dividends, and debt repayments.
